element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanics tutorial provided by NPCs
Primary goal reinforced throughout; percentage scoring at level end
Enemy drops; used in shops for upgrades and level skips

element_introduction_order.element: Run ability (move faster like a dog)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Limited ammo variants; replace default club
Collecting all five unlocks bonus area; rewards bones and food
Spend bones for items, level skips, save game functionality
Level-specific gameplay variant requiring new control scheme
Exploration-focused level type; specific items required to progress

typical_relief_triggers: Collecting heart containers to increase health pool
Accessing bonus areas after collecting B-O-N-U-S letters
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy encounters requiring bone currency for progression
Limited ammo on alternative weapons forcing decisions
Vehicle-based level gimmicks with unfamiliar controls
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Variable rhythm controlled by level gimmicks: straightforward platformer levels alternate with specialized challenge types (boss fights, vehicle segments, key hunts) to maintain engagement; checkpoint system provides mid-level relief point; percentage-based scoring encourages replay and exploration

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: B-O-N-U-S letters hidden throughout levels; heart containers and shields found off main path; shops positioned as optional detours

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with basic platforming and club combat; NPCs provide tutorial hints; simple enemy types and level layouts establish core mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of alternative weapons, abilities (shout, run), and level gimmicks; shops and permanent upgrades become available; exploration for B-O-N-U-S letters increases complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss-heavy; vehicle-based challenges; key-item hunts; limited ammo management becomes critical; health pools from collected containers essential for survival
notable_spikes: First boss encounter (transition from simple platforming to combat-heavy challenge)
Introduction of vehicle gimmicks (hang glider levels require new spatial reasoning)
Late-game boss gauntlet (health management and weapon strategy critical)
Final levels combining multiple gimmick types simultaneously

notable_level_patterns.description: Majority of levels; side-scrolling traversal with enemy encounters, food collection, checkpoint midway, percentage scoring system. Foundational level type that establishes pacing baseline
Level-end encounters with unique mechanics and higher difficulty; requires accumulated health from heart containers and strategic weapon/ability use
Specialized challenge restricting player to vehicle (hang glider documented); unfamiliar control scheme creates distinct tension; limited to subset of level count
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Hang glider level (specific level number not documented)

notable_level_patterns.description: Exploration-focused variant requiring player to locate specific items to proceed; emphasizes spatial discovery over combat
Unlocked by collecting all B-O-N-U-S letters (5 per game); rewards-focused level providing concentrated bone and food pickups; optional but incentivized

sources_used: Wikipedia - Prehistorik Man (gameplay mechanics, level count, abilities, items, boss/vehicle/key-hunt level types, difficulty progression)
TCRF - Prehistorik Man SNES (16 continues available, game classification as 'mediocre platformer')
